sections.heading: GovSecure AI Policy Suite Map
paragraphs: 15 Core Enterprise AI Policies with Purpose, Ownership, Audience, and Companion Templates
Version 1.0
tables: Client-Ready Working Document
Designed to help organizations structure enterprise AI governance, privacy, security, risk review, and operational accountability.
Recommended use: policy architecture, client delivery, governance planning, product packaging, and implementation workshops.
Prepared for GovSecure

sections.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Purpose. This document defines the primary enterprise AI policies recommended for a formal AI governance program and maps each policy to its purpose, primary owner, intended audience, and companion templates required for operational use.

How to use this document. Use this suite map as a policy architecture guide, a client-facing planning document, or the backbone for a GovSecure policy product. It is designed to move organizations from loose AI guidance to a structured, accountable, and implementation-ready policy set.

Recommended packaging tiers. Tier 1 covers the starter set for organizations beginning formal AI governance. Tier 2 adds operational control depth. Tier 3 adds maturity, assurance, and audit readiness.

sections.heading: Policy Suite Summary Matrix
tables: Policy
Purpose Snapshot
Primary Owner
Audience
Key Templates
Enterprise AI Acceptable Use Policy
Rules for day-to-day employee use of AI tools and outputs.
AI Governance Lead with Security and Legal support
Enterprise workforce and approved users
Approved AI Tools Register; Employee AI Usage Acknowledgment Form; Prohibited Use Exception Request Form...
AI Governance Policy
Defines ownership, oversight, escalation, and decision rights.
Executive Sponsor / AI Governance Lead
Leaders and control functions
AI Governance Charter; AI Governance RACI Matrix; AI Steering Committee Terms of Reference...
AI Data Handling and Privacy Policy
Defines how AI-related data is classified, used, retained, and protected.
Privacy Lead / Data Protection Lead with Security and Data Governance support
AI users and data/control owners
AI Data Classification Guide; Sensitive Data Submission Restriction Notice; AI Privacy Impact Assessment Form...
AI Risk Assessment and Use-Case Approval Policy
Requires AI use cases to be reviewed before adoption or material change.
AI Governance Lead with Risk, Legal, Privacy, and Security review support

AI Use Case Intake Form; AI Risk Tiering Worksheet; AI Approval Decision Record...
Third-Party / Vendor AI Due Diligence Policy
Sets requirements for evaluating AI vendors and service providers.
Procurement / Vendor Risk Lead with Security, Privacy, and Legal support
Procurement and review teams
Third-Party AI Privacy Risk Assessment; AI Vendor Security Questionnaire; AI Vendor Contract Review Checklist...
AI Security Policy
Defines technical and operational security requirements for AI systems.
Information Security Lead
Security, IT, engineering
AI Security Control Baseline; AI Access Control Review Form; AI API and Integration Security Checklist...
Human Oversight and Decision-Making Policy
Defines where humans must review or override AI-assisted actions.

AI Transparency and Disclosure Policy
Explains when AI use and AI-generated content must be disclosed.

AI Incident Response Policy
Defines how the organization handles AI-related incidents and harms.

AI Monitoring, Logging, and Change Management Policy
Requires monitoring, drift review, and approval of material AI changes.

AI Monitoring Standard; AI Logging Requirements Matrix; AI Drift Review Record...
AI Inventory and Registry Policy
Requires all enterprise AI systems and use cases to be inventoried.

AI Model Lifecycle / Development Policy
Applies when the enterprise builds, fine-tunes, or materially customizes models.

Defines fairness, accountability, transparency, and non-discrimination principles.

AI Training and Awareness Policy
Requires role-based training and awareness for AI users and overseers.
AI Governance Lead with HR and Security Awareness support
Entire workforce by role
AI Training Curriculum Matrix; Role-Based Training Assignment Sheet; AI Policy Acknowledgment Form...
AI Records Retention and Evidence Policy
Defines what governance and control evidence must be retained.
Records Management / Compliance Lead with Privacy, Legal, and Governance support
Compliance, audit, governance
AI Evidence Register; AI Governance Document Index; Retention Schedule Matrix...

sections.heading: Tier 1 - Core Starter Set

bullets: AI Monitoring, Logging, and Change Management Policy
sections.heading: Tier 2 - Operational Control Set

bullets: AI Records Retention and Evidence Policy
sections.heading: Tier 3 - Maturity / Assurance Set
1. Enterprise AI Acceptable Use Policy
tables: Purpose

Sets the rules for everyday employee use of AI tools and AI-generated outputs. It defines approved use, prohibited use, data-entry restrictions, output review expectations, and baseline conduct requirements.
Primary Owner
AI Governance Lead with Security and Legal support
Audience
All employees, contractors, temporary workers, and approved third parties using enterprise AI tools.

sections.heading: Required companion templates
paragraphs: Implementation note. This is usually the first AI policy to launch because it governs frontline behavior immediately.

sections.heading: 2. AI Governance Policy
tables: Purpose
Defines the governance structure for AI across the enterprise, including ownership, oversight, approval authority, escalation, risk accountability, and review responsibilities.
Primary Owner
Executive Sponsor / AI Governance Lead
Audience
Executive leadership, business owners, Security, Privacy, Legal, IT, Procurement, Risk, and Compliance functions.

bullets: AI Steering Committee Terms of Reference
sections.heading: Required companion templates
paragraphs: Implementation note. Without this policy, the rest of the AI policy stack can become disconnected and weakly owned.

sections.heading: 3. AI Data Handling and Privacy Policy
tables: Purpose
Defines what data may be used in AI systems, what data is restricted or prohibited, how AI-related data should be classified, retained, deleted, transferred, and protected.
Primary Owner
Privacy Lead / Data Protection Lead with Security and Data Governance support
Audience
All employees using AI, data owners, system owners, Privacy, Security, Legal, and procurement teams.

bullets: AI Data Retention and Deletion Schedule
Cross-Border AI Data Transfer Review Form
sections.heading: Required companion templates
paragraphs: Implementation note. This policy becomes essential the moment employees may enter business, customer, or personal data into AI tools.

sections.heading: 4. AI Risk Assessment and Use-Case Approval Policy
tables: Purpose
Requires AI use cases to be reviewed before adoption, procurement, deployment, or material change. Establishes risk tiers, approval triggers, prohibited use cases, and enhanced review criteria.
Primary Owner
AI Governance Lead with Risk, Legal, Privacy, and Security review support
Audience
Business owners, project sponsors, AI requestors, procurement teams, IT, Security, Privacy, and Legal.

sections.heading: Required companion templates
paragraphs: Implementation note. This is the gatekeeping policy that stops shadow AI from maturing into unmanaged enterprise risk.

sections.heading: 5. Third-Party / Vendor AI Due Diligence Policy
tables: Purpose
Establishes requirements for assessing third-party AI vendors, platforms, models, and service providers before onboarding, renewal, or major scope change.
Primary Owner
Procurement / Vendor Risk Lead with Security, Privacy, and Legal support
Audience
Procurement, Vendor Management, Security, Privacy, Legal, business sponsors, and AI governance reviewers.

sections.heading: Required companion templates
paragraphs: Implementation note. This policy is especially important because many AI risks are inherited from providers, not internally created.

sections.heading: 6. AI Security Policy
tables: Purpose

Defines security requirements for enterprise AI tools, models, APIs, agents, integrations, credentials, logging, and access. It also addresses misuse, abuse, prompt injection, data leakage, and security hardening.
Primary Owner
Information Security Lead
Audience
Security teams, IT administrators, developers, engineers, platform owners, and business owners using privileged AI capabilities.

bullets: AI API and Integration Security Checklist
Prompt Injection and Abuse Testing Checklist
sections.heading: Required companion templates
paragraphs: Implementation note. This policy should connect tightly to existing enterprise security standards rather than float as a standalone technical memo.

sections.heading: 7. Human Oversight and Decision-Making Policy
tables: Purpose
Defines when human review is required before AI outputs are acted upon, especially for legal, financial, employment, safety, health, and customer-impacting decisions.
Primary Owner
AI Governance Lead with Legal and business owner support
Audience
Business decision-makers, managers, operations teams, HR, Legal, compliance reviewers, and customer-facing teams.

sections.heading: Required companion templates
paragraphs: Implementation note. This policy is one of the clearest ways to make AI governance real instead of theoretical.

sections.heading: 8. AI Transparency and Disclosure Policy
tables: Purpose
Defines when the enterprise must disclose AI use to employees, customers, partners, applicants, or other stakeholders, and when AI-generated content must be labeled or explained.
Primary Owner
Legal / Compliance with Communications and AI Governance support
Audience
Legal, Compliance, Marketing, HR, Customer Support, Product teams, and business owners.

sections.heading: Required companion templates
paragraphs: Implementation note. This policy becomes highly visible externally, so it should be plain-language and consistent with customer-facing commitments.

sections.heading: 9. AI Incident Response Policy
tables: Purpose
Defines what qualifies as an AI incident and how the organization detects, escalates, investigates, contains, remediates, and learns from AI-related failures or harms.
Primary Owner
Information Security Lead with Privacy, Legal, IT, and business owner support
Audience
Security operations, Privacy, Legal, IT, risk teams, executives, incident responders, and system owners.

bullets: AI Harm and Impact Assessment Sheet
sections.heading: Required companion templates
paragraphs: Implementation note. AI incidents are not just security incidents; they can include privacy harm, fairness failures, and damaging automated behavior.

sections.heading: 10. AI Monitoring, Logging, and Change Management Policy
tables: Purpose
Requires ongoing monitoring of AI performance, drift, misuse, configuration changes, prompt changes, model/provider changes, and operational review cycles.
Primary Owner
IT / Platform Owner with Security and AI Governance oversight
Audience
IT, Security, platform teams, engineering, business owners, and governance reviewers.

sections.heading: Required companion templates
paragraphs: Implementation note. This policy turns AI governance from a one-time approval event into a living control process.

sections.heading: 11. AI Inventory and Registry Policy
tables: Purpose
Requires all enterprise AI tools, use cases, agents, and relevant systems to be inventoried with clear ownership, purpose, data types, risk tier, and status.
Primary Owner
AI Governance Lead with IT support
Audience
Business owners, IT, Security, Procurement, Privacy, Legal, internal audit, and risk teams.

sections.heading: Required companion templates
paragraphs: Implementation note. If the organization cannot inventory AI, it cannot govern AI. Simple and painful.

sections.heading: 12. AI Model Lifecycle / Development Policy
tables: Purpose
Applies where the enterprise builds, fine-tunes, configures materially, or customizes models. Covers model design, validation, release, rollback, documentation, and retirement.
Primary Owner
Engineering / Product Owner with Security and Governance oversight
Audience
Engineering, data science, product teams, model owners, security teams, and governance reviewers.

sections.heading: Required companion templates
paragraphs: Implementation note. Not every SMB needs this on day one, but any company building or heavily customizing AI will need it quickly.

sections.heading: 13. Responsible AI / Ethics Policy
tables: Purpose
Defines the enterprise's principles for fairness, accountability, transparency, explainability, contestability, inclusion, and non-discrimination in AI use.
Primary Owner
Executive Sponsor / AI Governance Lead with Legal and HR support
Audience
Executives, product teams, HR, Legal, Compliance, customer-facing teams, and innovation teams.

sections.heading: Required companion templates
paragraphs: Implementation note. This policy gives the program a principles layer above operational controls and is useful for leadership communication and external trust.

sections.heading: 14. AI Training and Awareness Policy
tables: Purpose
Requires role-based training and awareness for enterprise AI usage, approvals, administration, development, and oversight responsibilities.
Primary Owner
AI Governance Lead with HR and Security Awareness support
Audience
All employees, managers, administrators, approvers, developers, and executives.

sections.heading: Required companion templates
paragraphs: Implementation note. Governance without training is paperwork with optimism attached.

sections.heading: 15. AI Records Retention and Evidence Policy
tables: Purpose
Defines what governance, approval, monitoring, vendor, and incident records must be retained to support compliance, audit, review, and accountability.
Primary Owner
Records Management / Compliance Lead with Privacy, Legal, and Governance support
Audience
Compliance, audit, Legal, AI governance, Security, IT, and business owners.

sections.heading: Required companion templates
paragraphs: Implementation note. This policy is where operational discipline becomes provable during audits, reviews, and client diligence exercises.

sections.heading: GovSecure Productization Recommendation
paragraphs: Recommended product name. GovSecure AI Policy Suite Map - 15 Core Enterprise AI Policies with Operational Templates

Commercial packaging approach. Offer the suite in layers: Lite (policy map + core templates), Standard (8 drafted policies + template pack), and Pro (full 15-policy suite + questionnaires + registry + evidence pack).
Positioning note. The value is not only in the policy documents themselves but in the operational templates that make the policies usable, reviewable, and auditable.

sections.heading: Closing Note
paragraphs: This suite map is designed to help organizations stand up an AI governance program that is practical, role-aware, and scalable. It can serve as a planning document, a client-ready advisory deliverable, or the backbone of a GovSecure product offering.
